blurb: Blue-green scalloped leaves rimmed in rose-purple on sprawling, branching stems, colouring up hardest at the edges in bright light. Sprouts tiny plantlets right along the leaf notches.

care.light: Bright light with some direct sun brings out the purple rims and keeps growth compact; in shade it greens and stretches.
care.water: Soak-and-dry; it stores water in the leaves, so let the mix dry fully between drinks. Drier in winter.
care.temperature: Room temperature; keep above ~40°F and out of frost.
care.dormancy: A warm-season grower that slows in winter — keep it bright and on the dry side then.
care.feeding: A dilute feed once or twice through spring and summer.
care.potting: Repot as it sprawls; pinch back and re-root cuttings to keep it bushy.
care.propagation: Very easy — stem cuttings, and the little leaf-margin plantlets that root wherever they land.
care.bloom: Nodding bell-shaped orange-red flowers on tall stalks in winter to spring.
care.troubles: Overwatering rot and legginess in low light; mealybugs and aphids on new growth.
care.toxicity: Toxic — Kalanchoe contain cardiac glycosides that are dangerous to cats and dogs. Keep it out of reach.
